director.name: 帕洛玛·施奈德曼 Paloma Schneideman

introduction: Set in a liminal, alien terrain, Faceless Hair Cry is an experimental dance film that charts the cyclical metamorphosis of a creature born from fluid, flowers, and flesh. As the creature learns its body through silk-like movement—crawling, writhing, seducing, and shedding, it becomes more self-aware, and undergoes a series of physical and psychological transformations: discovering its sexuality, wrestling with voyeurism, and gorging itself to grow a new exoskeleton made entirely of hair. Through cyclical acts of rebirth, Faceless Hair Cry explores themes of fertility, identity, and the tension between nature and artifice.
